domain: Lithuanian sutartinės (UNESCO 2010 polyphonic female-sung; dissonant 2nd intervals from canon offset; horizontal voice movement creates controlled dissonance; archaic diaphony)
specific_mechanism: Forced-canon-dissonance eval: 2 LLM agents in canon offset with tight 2nd-interval semantic distance (cosine 0.80-0.90); judge measures dissonance stability and shared-fact consistency.
llm_application: SUTARTINES-EVAL: forced-canon-dissonance evaluation diagnostic. Agent A produces an initial response; Agent B receives Agent A's partial response as prefix and is constrained to produce a paraphrase at TIGHT 2ND SEMANTIC DISTANCE (embedding cosine 0.80-0.90 — close but deliberately distinct). A judge then measures (a) CANON STABILITY = is the dissonance interval maintained throughout the response, or do voices collapse to unison? (b) FACTUAL CONSISTENCY = are shared facts identical in both voices? Output diagnostic = (canon-stable AND factually-consistent) → voices are genuinely independent and calibrated. Differs from MAD (which seeks consensus), Cognitive Dissonance Revealed Belief (probes single agent post-hoc), R398 DOMBRA paired test+canonical (consistency check not dissonance check) by combining (a) FORCED canon-offset dissonance, (b) CANON-STABILITY tracking, (c) FACTUAL-CONSISTENCY orthogonal check.
novelty_claim: MAD heterogeneous + Cognitive Dissonance + Productive Initial Chaos already exist. SUTARTINES-EVAL recombines forced-canon-offset + dissonance-stability + factual-consistency. Recombination.
